# openwrt-docs4ai - Complete Flat Catalog
> Includes generated AI-facing helper surfaces and all published L2 source documents.
> **Total Underlying L2 Context:** ~439660 tokens

- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): AI agent usage rules for navigating the generated OpenWrt documentation corpus. (~468 tokens, l3-agent-guide)
- [architecture-overview.md](./cookbook/chunked-reference/architecture-overview.md): Structural overview of OpenWrt component relationships, data flow, ACL/permission boundaries, and build-time vs runtime distinctions for AI tools and developers building on the platform. (~2981 tokens, l2-source)
- [common-ai-mistakes.md](./cookbook/chunked-reference/common-ai-mistakes.md): Documents seven recurring categories of era-mismatched or API-incorrect patterns that AI tools produce when generating OpenWrt code, with WRONG/CORRECT pairs for each. (~2642 tokens, l2-source)
- [luci-form-with-uci.md](./cookbook/chunked-reference/luci-form-with-uci.md): End-to-end guide to creating a LuCI settings page that reads and writes UCI configuration, covering the JavaScript view, form.Map binding, rpcd plugin, ACL file, and the complete request flow from browser to disk. (~3159 tokens, l2-source)
- [minimal-openwrt-package-makefile.md](./cookbook/chunked-reference/minimal-openwrt-package-makefile.md): Annotated minimal Makefile for packaging a C program in OpenWrt, covering the required variables, Build/Compile, Package/install, and the correct PKG_HASH vs PKG_MD5SUM distinction. (~2639 tokens, l2-source)
- [openwrt-era-guide.md](./cookbook/chunked-reference/openwrt-era-guide.md): Reference for identifying which OpenWrt era (legacy, transitional, or current) a given pattern belongs to, so AI tools produce era-appropriate recommendations. (~2653 tokens, l2-source)
- [procd-service-lifecycle.md](./cookbook/chunked-reference/procd-service-lifecycle.md): Complete guide to writing a procd-managed init script for an OpenWrt service, covering the full lifecycle from start to reload to stop with supervised respawn. (~2130 tokens, l2-source)
- [uci-read-write-from-ucode.md](./cookbook/chunked-reference/uci-read-write-from-ucode.md): Concrete guide to reading and writing UCI configuration from a ucode script or rpcd plugin, covering cursor lifecycle, get/set/commit, section iteration, and error handling. (~2648 tokens, l2-source)
- [example_app-luci-app-commands-htdocs-luci-static-resources-view-commands-js.md](./luci-examples/chunked-reference/example_app-luci-app-commands-htdocs-luci-static-resources-view-commands-js.md): The commands.js module in the luci-examples application provides a web interface for configuring custom shell commands in OpenWrt. (~247 tokens, l2-source)
- [example_app-luci-app-commands-ucode-controller-commands-uc.md](./luci-examples/chunked-reference/example_app-luci-app-commands-ucode-controller-commands-uc.md): The commands.uc module in the luci-examples application provides functionality to parse command-line arguments and execute commands within the OpenWrt environment. (~1625 tokens, l2-source)
- [example_app-luci-app-ddns-htdocs-luci-static-resources-view-ddns-overview-js.md](./luci-examples/chunked-reference/example_app-luci-app-ddns-htdocs-luci-static-resources-view-ddns-overview-js.md): The overview.js module is part of the luci-examples application in OpenWrt, designed to manage Dynamic DNS (DDNS) services. (~11218 tokens, l2-source)

- [wiki_page-guide-developer-jshn.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-jshn.md): The jshn module is a JSON parsing and generation library designed for use in shell scripts within OpenWrt. (~3692 tokens, l2-source)

- [wiki_page-guide-developer-network-scripting.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-network-scripting.md): The Network scripts module in OpenWrt provides a framework for implementing protocol handlers that enable various network configurations through the netifd daemon. - [wiki_page-guide-developer-obtain-firmware-sdk.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-obtain-firmware-sdk.md): The SDK (Software Development Kit) for OpenWrt is a relocatable, precompiled toolchain designed for cross-compiling single userspace packages for specific targets without the need to compile the entire system. - [wiki_page-guide-developer-write-shell-script.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-guide-developer-write-shell-script.md): The OpenWrt operating system utilizes the Almquist shell (ash), which is part of the BusyBox suite, to provide a lightweight and efficient command-line interface for embedded devices. (~1431 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-architecture.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-architecture.md): OpenWrt's operating system architecture is designed to be lightweight and efficient, utilizing components like libubox, ubus, and procd instead of the larger libraries commonly found in desktop distributions. - [wiki_page-techref-bootloader.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-bootloader.md): The Bootloader is a critical piece of software executed upon powering up a hardware device, responsible for initializing low-level hardware details and passing a hardware description to the Kernel. (~2139 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-brcm63xx-imagetag.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-brcm63xx-imagetag.md): The BCM63xx Firmware Image Information module provides tools and details for analyzing firmware image tags specific to Broadcom 63xx devices. (~6807 tokens, l2-source)

(~1012 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-busybox.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-busybox.md): BusyBox is a crucial component in OpenWrt that consolidates various system utilities into a single binary, providing lightweight alternatives to many standard Unix tools. (~676 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-dfs.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-dfs.md): DFS Dynamic_frequency_selection plays a role in 5GHz frequencies that are shared with Terminal_Doppler_Weather_Radar. (~694 tokens, l2-source)

- [wiki_page-techref-file-system.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-file-system.md): The OpenWrt File System Hierarchy and Memory Usage module provides a detailed overview of how flash storage is partitioned and utilized in OpenWrt devices, specifically using the TP-Link WR1043ND as an example. (~1566 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-filesystems-network.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-filesystems-network.md): The Network Filesystems module in OpenWrt allows the system to mount various filesystems over an IP network, functioning as both a client and a server. (~330 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-filesystems.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-filesystems.md): The Filesystems module in OpenWrt provides an overview of various file systems utilized for device built-in flash storage. (~3337 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-flash-layout.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-flash-layout.md): The OpenWrt Flash Layout module provides an overview of the flash memory types used in embedded devices targeted by OpenWrt, specifically focusing on the distinctions between raw flash and FTL flash, as well as NOR and NAND flash types. (~9839 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-flash.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-flash.md): The Flash memory module in OpenWrt provides comprehensive insights into the considerations and types of flash memory used in embedded devices. (~3087 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-headers.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-headers.md): The TRX, TRX2, and BIN firmware formats are used in various OpenWrt devices, each having distinct header structures despite containing similar content. (~1469 tokens, l2-source)

(~5589 tokens, l2-source)

(~2388 tokens, l2-source)

(~732 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-libnl.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-libnl.md): The libnl library facilitates communication with the kernel through netlink sockets, allowing applications to manage routing information and interface settings. (~545 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-libubox.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-libubox.md): Documents libubox, the OpenWrt utility library that underpins ubus, procd, and netifd. (~748 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-lldpd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-lldpd.md): lldpd See also: lldpd upstream documentation The goal of LLDP is to provide an inter-vendor compatible mechanism to deliver Link-Layer notifications to adjacent network devices. (~380 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-luci.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-luci.md): Provides a technical overview of the LuCI web interface framework for OpenWrt. (~1499 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-luci2.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-luci2.md): LuCI2 is an experimental web user interface for OpenWrt, designed to replace the older Lua-based LuCI. (~1821 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-mountd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-mountd.md): The mountd module is an automount daemon for OpenWrt, configured through the /etc/config/mountd file. (~323 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-mtd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-mtd.md): The mtd utility is designed for writing to Memory Technology Devices (MTD) in OpenWrt. (~1589 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-netifd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-netifd.md): Documents netifd, the OpenWrt network interface daemon responsible for managing network interfaces based on UCI config. (~1929 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-odhcpd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-odhcpd.md): odhcpd is a daemon designed for managing DHCP, DHCPv6, Router Advertisements (RA), and Neighbor Discovery Protocol (NDP) in embedded systems, particularly for IPv6 home routers. (~2900 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-preinit-mount.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-preinit-mount.md): The OpenWrt preinit and root mount system is responsible for the initial boot sequence and firstboot scripts that prepare the system for multiuser operation. (~12192 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-procd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-procd.md): Describes procd, the OpenWrt process manager and init system that replaces busybox init. (~1963 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-process-boot.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-process-boot.md): The Boot Process module provides a comprehensive overview of the sequence of operations that occur when an OpenWrt device is powered on, starting from the bootloader to the initialization of user space. (~3243 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-requirements-boot-process.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-requirements-boot-process.md): The Boot/Init Requirements document outlines the necessary functions and goals for initscripts in the new OpenWrt init system. (~1562 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-rpcd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-rpcd.md): Documents rpcd, the OpenWrt RPC daemon that exposes ubus methods over HTTP for LuCI. (~1043 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-swconfig.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-swconfig.md): The swconfig program is used for configuring Ethernet network switches in OpenWrt. (~3222 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-sysupgrade.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-sysupgrade.md): The Sysupgrade module in OpenWrt is a shell script located at /sbin/sysupgrade designed to facilitate system updates. (~2605 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-ubox.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-ubox.md): The ubox package is a component of the OpenWrt operating system that facilitates the management of UCI configurations and block devices. (~242 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-ubus.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-ubus.md): Explains the OpenWrt ubus inter-process communication bus, which provides a Unix-socket-based RPC mechanism for daemons to expose services and receive method calls. (~6656 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-uci.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-uci.md): Documents the OpenWrt Unified Configuration Interface (UCI) — the standard system for storing and modifying router configuration. (~3923 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-ucwmp.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-ucwmp.md): EasyCwmp is a client implementation of the TR-069 CPE WAN Management Protocol for OpenWrt, developed by PIVA Software and licensed under GPL 2. (~943 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-unetd.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-unetd.md): unetd is a WireGuard based VPN daemon designed for OpenWrt routers, facilitating the creation and management of fully-meshed VPN connections. (~3162 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-wireless-modes.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-wireless-modes.md): The Wireless Modes module in OpenWrt outlines various wireless operation modes supported by the system, including Access Point (AP), Ad-Hoc (IBSS), and Mesh Point (802.11s). (~349 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-wireless-standards.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-wireless-standards.md): The Wireless Standards module in OpenWrt provides an overview of various wireless communication standards relevant to the operating system. (~845 tokens, l2-source)
- [wiki_page-techref-xenomai.md](./wiki/chunked-reference/wiki_page-techref-xenomai.md): The Xenomai module provides a real-time framework within OpenWrt, allowing for the handling of interrupts in a prioritized manner through Adeos. (~502 tokens, l2-source)
